description: Unexpected var, use let or const instead.

content.body: Variables declared with `var` are function-scoped and hoisted, which can lead to subtle bugs. Use `let` for mutable variables or `const` for immutable bindings.

description: Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

content.body: Using the `any` type disables TypeScript's static type checking. Consider using `unknown` with a type guard, or define a proper interface.
The `==` operator performs type coercion before comparison, which can produce unexpected results. Always use `===` for strict equality checks.

description: Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

content.body: Define a proper `User` interface with the expected properties instead of using `any` as the parameter type.
Explicitly specifying return types improves code readability and helps TypeScript catch type errors earlier in the compilation pipeline.

description: Function 'verifyToken' has a cyclomatic complexity of 3. Maximum allowed is 2.

content.body: High cyclomatic complexity makes code harder to test and maintain. Consider extracting conditional logic into small, well-named helper functions.
console.log statements should not be present in production code. Use a structured logger (e.g. pino, winston) with appropriate log levels instead.

description: Promises must be awaited, end with a call to .catch, or end with a call to .then with a rejection handler.

content.body: Unhandled promise rejections can cause silent failures in Express route handlers. Ensure all async operations are properly awaited and errors are caught and forwarded to next().

description: Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

content.body: Use the built-in `Error` type or define a custom error interface instead of `any` for the error parameter.
Remove unused function declarations to keep the codebase clean, or export the function if it is intended to be used by other modules.

description: Template literal containing SQL keyword — use a parameterised query instead.

content.body: Interpolating user-controlled values into SQL strings enables SQL injection. Use a parameterised query: db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [id]).

description: Template literal containing SQL keyword — use a parameterised query instead.

content.body: Interpolating user-controlled values into a DELETE statement enables SQL injection. An attacker can delete arbitrary rows or bypass WHERE conditions entirely.

description: Template literal containing SQL keyword — use a parameterised query instead.

content.body: Interpolating user-controlled values into a DELETE statement enables SQL injection. An attacker can delete arbitrary rows or bypass WHERE conditions entirely.
